spec_match.rationale: Tooltip's top inset is `calc(anchor(bottom) + anchor-size(--target height) * 0.25)` and margin-right is `calc(anchor-size(--target width) * 0.1)` — anchor-size() in BOTH inset and margin, the exact spec change.

interactive.rationale: Slider rescales the target font-size; tooltip offset rescales declaratively via CSS without JS measurement.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a slider-driven scaling tooltip offset; that is the deliverable.

edge_cases.rationale: No fallback for pre-131 browsers, no readout of computed values (visitor cannot verify the offset really is 25% of height), and the tooltip overlaps a fixed-height stage so at large font sizes the tooltip can overflow the container — never visually flagged.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables used throughout, --accent-blue/--bg-ivory pairing for tooltip passes WCAG AA, --text-black/--bg-ivory for target also pass.

openQuestions.title: Surface the computed offset live
openQuestions.detail: Right now the visitor sees the tooltip move but cannot verify the maths. Add a `<output>` showing `getComputedStyle(tooltip).top` in pixels, updated on each input event. Makes the spec change concrete instead of theoretical.

openQuestions.title: Show what happens with no font-size transition
openQuestions.detail: The lede notes 'before v131 the JS resize event broke under transitions'. Add a toggle: 'animate target' vs 'snap target' to highlight the difference. With the CSS approach, both work; with JS, only the snap version does.

summary: Resolved the required ChromeStatus reference; 2 follow-up question(s) remain.
